title: Reference Equations for the Six-Minute Walk Distance in Healthy Chinese Adults
author: He Zou
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.i9sch6e

steps.title: The six-minute walk test (6MWT) should...
steps.instruction: The six-minute walk test (6MWT) should be performed indoors, along a long, flat, straight, enclosed corridor with a hard surface that is seldom traveled. If the weather is comfortable, the test may be performed outdoors. The walking course must be 30 m in length.

(1). Countdown timer (or stopwatch)(2). Mechanical lap counter(3). Two small cones to mark the turnaround points(4). A chair that can be easily moved along the walking course(5). Worksheets on a clipboard(6). A oximeter(7). Sphygmomanometer(8). Telephone

steps.title: Appropriate shoes for walking should be worn
steps.instruction: Appropriate shoes for walking should be worn.

steps.title: A light meal is acceptable before...
steps.instruction: A light meal is acceptable before early morning or early afternoon tests.

steps.title: Subjects should not have exercised vigorously...
steps.instruction: Subjects should not have exercised vigorously within 2 hours of beginning the test.

steps.title: Repeat testing should be performed about...
steps.instruction: Repeat testing should be performed about the same time of day to minimize intraday variability.

steps.title: A “warm-up” period before the test should not be performed
steps.instruction: A “warm-up” period before the test should not be performed.

steps.title: The subject should sit at rest...
steps.instruction: The subject should sit at rest in a chair, located near the starting position, for at least 10 minutes before the test starts. During this time, check for contraindications, measure pulse and blood pressure, and make sure that clothing and shoes are appropriate.

steps.title: Position the subject at the starting line
steps.instruction: Position the subject at the starting line. You should also stand near the starting line during the test. Each subject was asked to walk up and down the corridor as fast as possible in six minutes. As soon as the subject starts to walk, start the timer.

steps.title: The operator needed to prompted the...
steps.instruction: The operator needed to prompted the subjects every 60 seconds using standardized encouragement ('you are doing well; you have five minutes left', 'good job; there are four minutes left').

steps.title: Post-test
steps.instruction: Post-test: Record the postwalk Borg dyspnea and ask this: “What, if anything, kept you from walking farther?”

steps.title: If using a pulse oximeter, measure...
steps.instruction: If using a pulse oximeter, measure SpO2 and pulse rate from the oximeter and then remove the sensor.

steps.title: Record the number of laps from...
steps.instruction: Record the number of laps from the counter (or tick marks on the worksheet).

steps.title: Record the additional distance covered (the...
steps.instruction: Record the additional distance covered (the number of meters in the final partial lap) using the markers on the wall as distance guides. Calculate the total distance walked, rounding to the nearest meter, and record it on the worksheet.

steps.title: Congratulate the subject on good effort...
steps.instruction: Congratulate the subject on good effort and offer a drink of water.
